# BUG-002 Writeup
> Generated by [Quality Playbook](https://github.com/andrewstellman/quality-playbook) v1.4.6 — Andrew Stellman
> Date: 2026-04-20 · Project: virtio-1.4.6

## 1. Summary

REQ-003 requires config-triggered work to count as handled IRQ work, but `vp_interrupt()` in `drivers/virtio/virtio_pci_common.c:106-124` drops the handled result from `vp_config_changed()`.

## 2. Why this is a bug

The INTx handler can consume a real config interrupt, invoke driver-visible config handling, and still return `IRQ_NONE` when the regular virtqueue list had no work. That misclassifies a serviced interrupt as spurious.

## 3. Regression test

- **Requirement:** REQ-003
- **Test:** `quality/test_regression.py::TestCodeReviewRegressions.test_bug_002_vp_interrupt_preserves_config_handled_status`
- **Regression patch:** `quality/patches/BUG-002-regression-test.patch`

## 4. FAIL -> PASS evidence

```text
RED: Command: python3 <direct regression assertion runner> test_bug_002_vp_interrupt_preserves_config_handled_status
RED: AssertionError: 'return vp_vring_interrupt(irq, opaque);' unexpectedly found in vp_interrupt()
GREEN: Command: python3 <temp source patcher BUG-002> && python3 <direct regression assertion runner> test_bug_002_vp_interrupt_preserves_config_handled_status
GREEN: test_bug_002_vp_interrupt_preserves_config_handled_status: PASS
```

See `quality/results/BUG-002.red.log` and `quality/results/BUG-002.green.log`.

## 5. Challenge gate

`quality/challenge/BUG-002-challenge.md` -> **CONFIRMED**

## 6. The fix

- **Fix patch:** `quality/patches/BUG-002-fix.patch`

```diff
diff --git a/drivers/virtio/virtio_pci_common.c b/drivers/virtio/virtio_pci_common.c
index 2222222..3333333 100644
--- a/drivers/virtio/virtio_pci_common.c
+++ b/drivers/virtio/virtio_pci_common.c
@@ -106,6 +106,7 @@ static irqreturn_t vp_interrupt(int irq, void *opaque)
 {
 	struct virtio_pci_device *vp_dev = opaque;
 	u8 isr;
+	irqreturn_t ret = IRQ_NONE;
 
 	isr = ioread8(vp_dev->isr);
 	if (!isr)
@@ -119,5 +120,8 @@ static irqreturn_t vp_interrupt(int irq, void *opaque)
 	/* Configuration change?  Tell driver if it wants to know. */
 	if (isr & VIRTIO_PCI_ISR_CONFIG)
-		vp_config_changed(irq, opaque);
+		ret = vp_config_changed(irq, opaque);
 
-	return vp_vring_interrupt(irq, opaque);
+	ret |= vp_vring_interrupt(irq, opaque);
+
+	return ret;
 }
```
